He suggested the changes appear motivated by factors other than performance and expressed sadness over the potential loss of this American institution.

item.title: The 1915 Simplex Crane Model 5 Tourer and Its Role as a Premier American Luxury Automobile
item.description:
The article highlights the 1915 Simplex Crane Model 5 Tourer, one of the most prestigious and technologically advanced American automobiles of the Brass Era. Manufactured by the Simplex Automobile Company in New Brunswick, New Jersey, the vehicle represented the highest standards of luxury, performance, and craftsmanship available in the United States before World War I. At its core was a massive 9.7-liter inline-six engine with an output of approximately 110 horsepower, an extraordinary figure for the period that placed the car among the most powerful road vehicles of its time.
The Model 5 Tourer gained a reputation for exceptional engineering quality and elegant design. Its open touring body featured graceful proportions, large wooden-spoke wheels, and styling intended for comfortable long-distance travel. The automobile appealed primarily to wealthy industrialists, enthusiasts, and sportsmen who desired both speed and refinement. Its high purchase price placed it in direct competit

item.title: Former Trump White House Official Criticizes President's Focus on Reflecting Pool Renovation Amid Economic Concerns
item.description:
Former Trump White House deputy press secretary Sarah Matthews criticized President Donald Trump's continued focus on the renovation of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool during a CNN appearance. The discussion followed a montage of Trump praising the project and describing the landmark as a major achievement, including comments comparing it to 'a swimming pool on steroids' and emphasizing the scale of the renovation work.
Matthews acknowledged that Trump's enthusiasm for the project is consistent with his background in real estate development, arguing that construction and infrastructure projects are subjects that genuinely interest and excite him. She suggested that the president enjoys discussing visible building accomplishments more than complex policy matters.
However, Matthews argued that Trump's emphasis on the Reflecting Pool is disconnected from the concerns of many Americans. She described the focus as 'tone deaf,' contending that voters are more concerned about economic

item.title: USDA Secretary Discusses New World Screwworm Threat as Officials Respond to Texas Cattle Case
item.description:
Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins addressed concerns about the spread of the New World Screwworm during a congressional oversight hearing, describing the parasite as a significant challenge for the livestock industry. The New World Screwworm is a flesh-eating pest whose larvae feed on living tissue, posing a serious threat to cattle and other animals. Rollins explained that one of the primary control methods involves releasing large numbers of sterile flies to prevent reproduction of the pest population, a strategy that helped eliminate the parasite from the United States decades ago.
Lawmakers and agricultural leaders expressed concern about the parasite's reappearance. Senator Cindy Hyde-Smith characterized the confirmed cases as alarming, while Oklahoma political figure Charles McCall said cattle producers in his state were highly concerned about the potential economic and agricultural impacts.
The issue also sparked political debate online. Some commentators highlighted previo

item.title: Rep. Robert Garcia Criticizes Pam Bondi for Shifting Responsibility in Epstein Investigation Testimony
item.description:
Representative Robert Garcia, the ranking Democrat on the House Oversight Committee, criticized former Attorney General Pam Bondi during an appearance on MS NOW, accusing her of attempting to shift responsibility for problems in the Jeffrey Epstein investigation onto her former deputy, Todd Blanche. The discussion followed Bondi's recent testimony, in which she rejected claims that she was blaming Blanche for shortcomings in the investigation while simultaneously emphasizing that he managed the inquiry and oversaw key decisions.
Garcia argued that Bondi repeatedly referenced Blanche during her testimony and effectively assigned responsibility to him for numerous issues related to the handling of the Epstein case. According to Garcia, Bondi cited Blanche's role in managing redactions and other aspects of the investigation while avoiding accountability for controversial decisions herself. He also criticized her refusal to accept responsibility for the prison transfer arrangement involvi

item.title: Bondi Tells Congress DOJ Removed Epstein Island Photo After Staff Questioned Its Authenticity
item.description:
A newly released congressional transcript reveals that former Attorney General Pam Bondi provided a different explanation from the Justice Department's public account regarding the temporary removal of a photograph showing Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick with Jeffrey Epstein on Epstein's private island, Little Saint James. During a closed-door interview with the House Oversight Committee on May 29, Bondi stated that Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche or members of his staff believed the image might have been generated by artificial intelligence and therefore questioned its authenticity. According to Bondi, the photograph was removed but later restored after officials confirmed that it genuinely depicted Lutnick and his family.
The explanation differs from the one previously given by the Justice Department to reporters. DOJ spokesperson Natalie Baldassarre had said the image was removed during a routine review of files flagged for nudity, despite the photograph containing no nudity

item.title: Hunter Biden Criticizes Jake Tapper and Stephen Miller in a Series of Posts on X
item.description:
Hunter Biden drew attention on social media after publishing a series of posts on X in which he sharply criticized CNN anchor Jake Tapper and White House official Stephen Miller. The posts marked a continuation of his recent activity on the platform, where he has become increasingly vocal about political and media-related topics.
One of Biden's comments was directed at Stephen Miller, a longtime adviser to President Donald Trump. Responding to another user's remark, Biden insulted Miller with a blunt and profane statement. He also targeted Jake Tapper, comparing the journalist to the fictional character Brick Tamland from the comedy film 'Anchorman.' Biden's criticism came after comments Tapper made regarding former First Lady Jill Biden, whom Hunter referred to as his mother.
In another lengthy post, Biden argued that media attention was disproportionately focused on his family while overlooking business dealings and activities involving members of the Trump family. He referenced se

item.title: Nancy Pelosi clashes with LindellTV reporter during Capitol hallway exchange over January 6 claims
item.description:
Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi was involved in a heated exchange with a conservative journalist in the halls of the U.S. Capitol on Thursday. The confrontation began when Alison Steinberg, a correspondent for LindellTV — a media outlet founded by MyPillow CEO Mike Lindell — approached Pelosi and questioned her about the events surrounding January 6 and the deployment of the National Guard. Steinberg referenced a previous encounter in which Pelosi had reportedly told her to “shut up,” and pressed her again on similar claims.
Pelosi responded sharply, rejecting the framing of the questions and telling the reporter to “shut up again,” stating that she was “speaking lies.” As the exchange escalated, Pelosi disputed assertions about the National Guard, insisting that the president at the time did not agree to deploy it and accusing the reporter of spreading misinformation. The back-and-forth continued as Steinberg attempted to ask additional questions, including inquiries about Pelosi’s

item.title: Report Finds Nearly 100 Pardoned January 6 Defendants Later Faced New Criminal Charges
item.description: A report highlighted by Lawfare found that at least 97 individuals who received clemency from President Donald Trump for their involvement in the January 6, 2021 Capitol attack have since been charged with or convicted of additional crimes. According to the report, this represents more than one out of every sixteen people among the approximately 1,500 defendants who received pardons or other forms of clemency. The new offenses vary widely in severity, ranging from relatively minor violations such as trespassing, property damage, and possession of drug paraphernalia to serious felony allegations including grand larceny, stalking, fraud, threats against public officials, and plans to harm law enforcement officers. Some cases involve particularly severe crimes, including offenses against children that resulted in lengthy prison sentences. The report states that at least fourteen individuals faced charges related to sex crimes or child sexual abuse material, while at least six were accused

item.title: Targeted Cancer Therapies Are Extending Survival and Allowing More Patients to Live Longer With the Disease
item.description:
Advances in targeted cancer therapies and precision medicine are allowing a growing number of patients to live longer with cancer, according to experts and data presented at the American Society of Clinical Oncology meeting. The American Cancer Society estimates that about 18 million Americans who have ever been diagnosed with cancer are currently alive, while approximately 70% of cancer patients now survive at least five years after diagnosis, a significant improvement from previous decades. Researchers attribute much of this progress to a better understanding of cancer biology and the development of treatments designed to attack specific genetic mutations or proteins that drive tumor growth.
The article highlights the experiences of patients such as Cathy Smithwick and Michelle Vacca, who have lived with cancer for many years thanks to a combination of targeted therapies, hormone treatments, chemotherapy, and experimental drugs. Smithwick has managed breast and ovarian cancers for m

item.title: Study Identifies Genetic Similarities Between Cat and Human Cancers, Highlighting Potential for Dual Treatments
item.description: A new international study suggests that researching cancer in domestic cats could provide valuable insights into human cancers due to significant genetic similarities between the two species. Led by scientist Louise van der Weyden at the Wellcome Sanger Institute, researchers analyzed tumor samples from nearly 500 cats and found notable mutations in genes equivalent to those in humans. The gene FBXW7, a key tumor suppressor, was frequently altered in feline mammary tumors, mirroring patterns seen in human breast cancer where such mutations are linked to poorer outcomes. Cats share a substantial portion of their genetic code with humans, more than many other mammals, making them potentially strong models for studying diseases. Additionally, pet cats often share environments with their owners, exposing them to similar environmental carcinogens. The study, published in the journal Science, identified genetic parallels in cancers affecting the blood, bones, lungs, skin, gastrointestinal tr

item.title: GE Aerospace Completes Qualification of 600-Volt Power Electronics for Future U.S. Army Combat Vehicles
item.description: GE Aerospace has successfully completed qualification testing for two advanced high-voltage power electronics systems designed to support the U.S. Army’s transition toward more electrically powered combat vehicles. The systems, known as the High-Voltage Power Controller (HVPC) and the Unidirectional Converter (UDC), are now considered ready for production after meeting Army requirements. Low-rate initial production is expected to begin in 2026, with deliveries scheduled for 2027. These technologies enable military vehicles to move from the traditional 28-volt electrical architecture to a much more capable 600-volt system. The higher-voltage approach allows vehicles to generate, distribute, and manage significantly more electrical power while reducing energy losses, decreasing wiring weight, and improving overall efficiency. The Army's growing demand for onboard electrical power is driven by increasingly sophisticated equipment, including advanced sensors, communications systems, electr

item.title: Leaked Correspondence Suggests King Charles Had Concerns About Hosting Trump During 2025 State Visit
item.description: A report by The i Paper, cited by Raw Story, claims that leaked emails and messages indicate King Charles III privately expressed concerns about hosting U.S. President Donald Trump during his state visit to the United Kingdom in 2025. According to the report, the King's unease was linked primarily to Trump's and Vice President JD Vance's public treatment of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy during a February 2025 exchange in which Zelenskyy was criticized for allegedly not showing sufficient gratitude for American support. Sources within Whitehall reportedly told the newspaper that the monarch was reluctant to host Trump at that time because of these events and broader concerns regarding U.S. support for Ukraine amid the ongoing war. One source allegedly described the King as nervous about the visit, while another claimed he preferred not to host Trump at all. The report states that British officials worked to address these concerns because they feared that any perceived rejectio

item.title: Jennifer Aniston and Lisa Kudrow Reflect on ‘Friends,’ Discuss AI and Consider a Future Sitcom With Courteney Cox
item.description:
In a wide-ranging conversation for Variety, longtime friends and former 'Friends' co-stars Jennifer Aniston and Lisa Kudrow reunited to discuss their careers, memories from the iconic sitcom, and their current projects. Kudrow reflected on finally watching the full run of 'Friends,' admitting that she had avoided doing so for years because she felt uncomfortable watching herself on screen. After eventually viewing the series, she said it brought her happiness and renewed appreciation for the cast's performances and chemistry.
The actresses reminisced about filming before a live audience, memorable guest stars such as Brad Pitt, Bruce Willis, Julia Roberts, Alec Baldwin, and Sean Penn, and some of their favorite comedic moments from the series. They particularly recalled scenes involving Ross's bagpipes and Joey's famous 'moo point' line.
Kudrow also discussed the third and final season of HBO's 'The Comeback,' explaining how the idea emerged from conversations about what her characte

item.title: Analyst Says Marco Rubio Has Gained Favor With Trump Over JD Vance Amid Iran Policy Disputes
item.description:
A political analysis published by The Bulwark and reported by Raw Story argues that Secretary of State Marco Rubio currently appears to have a stronger standing with President Donald Trump than Vice President JD Vance as speculation grows about potential Republican leadership and presidential succession in 2028. According to editor Jonathan V. Last, recent developments surrounding U.S. policy toward Iran have contributed to a shift in Trump's perception of the two officials.
Last contends that Trump has become increasingly dissatisfied with Vance following unsuccessful negotiations involving Iran. He argues that Vance was placed in a difficult position by being tasked with negotiations under unfavorable circumstances, while media reports and political leaks suggested that Trump questioned Vance's influence and effectiveness. In contrast, Rubio is described as having benefited politically despite previously holding positions that Last characterizes as incorrect regarding the Iran confl

item.title: Pardoned January 6 Participant Assigned to Pentagon Special Operations Office
item.description: According to a report cited by The New York Times, Elias Irizarry, a participant in the January 6, 2021, Capitol riot who was later pardoned by President Donald Trump, has been hired to work at the Pentagon. Irizarry was 19 years old when he entered the U.S. Capitol through a broken window during the attack and reportedly carried a metal pole. Following his conviction related to the events of January 6, he received a presidential pardon from Trump in 2025. The report states that Irizarry has been assigned to the Pentagon's Special Operations and Low Intensity Conflict office, a department involved in matters such as counterterrorism, irregular warfare, and support for elite U.S. military special operations forces. Former Assistant Secretary of Defense Michael Lumpkin expressed concern about the appointment, noting that the office deals with highly sensitive national security matters and that similar hiring decisions would likely have faced significant scrutiny in previous administratio

item.title: Nottingham Forest Turn Down Manchester City's Initial Offer for Midfielder Elliot Anderson
item.description:
Nottingham Forest have reportedly rejected Manchester City's first offer for midfielder Elliot Anderson as the Premier League champions continue their efforts to strengthen their squad ahead of the summer transfer window. Anderson, 23, has emerged as one of City's top transfer targets, with reports suggesting that manager Pep Guardiola's side views him as a key addition for the upcoming season.
According to the report, Forest are not actively seeking to sell the midfielder and have set a very high valuation on the player. The club is believed to be demanding a transfer fee that exceeds the £105 million Arsenal paid for Declan Rice, highlighting how highly they rate Anderson and their reluctance to part with him. Forest's stance reflects both the player's importance to the team and the club's strong negotiating position.
Despite the rejection, Manchester City are expected to continue pursuing the deal and could return with an improved offer in an attempt to reach an agreement. City re

item.title: Former Senator Bill Cassidy Joins Democratic Challenge to Treasury Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description:
A legal challenge has been filed against the Trump administration's so-called Anti-Weaponization Fund, a Treasury Department program valued at approximately $1.776 billion. The fund was created following a settlement related to President Donald Trump's lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service over the unauthorized disclosure of his tax returns. According to critics, the fund is intended to compensate individuals who claim they were unfairly targeted by previous federal investigations and prosecutions, including some Trump supporters and participants connected to the January 6, 2021 Capitol attack.
Former Republican Senator Bill Cassidy of Louisiana, who lost his party primary to a Trump-backed challenger, joined Democratic Senator Cory Booker of New Jersey in submitting an amicus brief opposing the program in the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Virginia. The filing argues that the fund exceeds executive authority and infringes upon Congress's constitutional power o

item.title: Questions Raised Over Tommy Tuberville's Residency After Records Show He Voted in Florida in 2018
item.description: A report by AL.com columnist Kyle Whitmire has renewed scrutiny over U.S. Senator Tommy Tuberville's residency history and his eligibility to run for governor of Alabama. The investigation focuses on whether Tuberville met Alabama's residency requirements after records indicated he voted in Florida in November 2018 despite tax documents suggesting he had reestablished residency in Alabama three months earlier. Tuberville owned a multimillion-dollar beach house in Florida for many years and purchased a smaller home in Auburn, Alabama, in 2017, which he later identified as his primary residence during his Senate campaign. According to tax records recently released by his campaign, Tuberville moved his residency back to Alabama in August 2018 and has maintained it there since then. However, Florida election records reportedly show that both Tuberville and his wife cast ballots in Florida during the 2018 election. The issue has gained political significance because Alabama law requires can

item.title: Fox News Poll Shows Declining Trump Approval Ratings in Ohio Ahead of Midterm Elections
item.description:
A new Fox News poll highlighted by CNN anchor Dana Bash suggests that President Donald Trump may be facing growing political challenges in Ohio as the 2026 midterm elections approach. According to the poll, Trump's approval rating in the traditionally Republican-leaning state has fallen to 42%, while 57% of respondents disapprove of his performance. Bash described the results as a significant warning sign for both the president and Republican candidates running in Ohio.
The poll indicates a notable deterioration in Trump's standing compared with previous measurements. Bash pointed out that the gap between approval and disapproval has widened considerably, creating what she characterized as a potentially serious electoral problem for Republicans. Because Ohio has historically been considered a reliable Republican state in recent national elections, such numbers could signal broader concerns about voter sentiment.
The survey also examined the Ohio Senate race. Republican Senator Jon Hu

item.title: Arizona Attorney General to Seek Fresh Indictment Against Trump Allies in 2020 Election Interference Case
item.description: Democratic Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes announced plans to pursue a new indictment against several allies of President Donald Trump who were involved in efforts to challenge the 2020 presidential election results in Arizona. This decision follows the Arizona Supreme Court's rejection of her appeal to revive the original two-year-old indictment. The high court issued a brief ruling without explanation, effectively ending the prior case that had targeted prominent figures including former White House Chief of Staff Mark Meadows, former New York Mayor Rudy Giuliani, attorney John Eastman, and 11 Arizona Republicans who acted as fake electors. The original charges accused the defendants of scheming to keep Trump in office against the will of Arizona voters by submitting false electoral certificates. Trump was named as an unindicted co-conspirator. Prosecutors alleged the group deceived the public by claiming their actions were contingent on legal challenges when their true intent wa

item.title: Rosalind Russell’s Rise to Hollywood Stardom During the 1930s
item.description:
This article highlights a collection of photographs documenting actress Rosalind Russell during the 1930s, a pivotal decade in her career. After beginning as a Broadway performer, Russell transitioned into Hollywood and became a contract player for Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer (MGM) in 1934. Although she initially struggled against typecasting and was often assigned supporting roles or parts rejected by more established stars such as Myrna Loy and Joan Crawford, she steadily built a reputation as a talented and versatile actress.
Russell made her official film debut in the mystery drama 'Evelyn Prentice' alongside William Powell and Myrna Loy. Throughout the decade, she demonstrated her dramatic abilities in films such as 'Craig’s Wife' (1936), where she portrayed a manipulative and obsessive housewife, as well as in 'Night Must Fall' (1937) and 'The Citadel' (1938). Her performance in 'West Point of the Air' (1935) earned significant critical recognition and showed that she could successfully

item.title: Conservative commentators criticize Candace Owens over appearance at Russia's St. Petersburg forum
item.description:
Conservative media personality Candace Owens has come under criticism from several prominent figures on the American right after attending the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum in Russia, an event widely viewed as being supported by the Kremlin. The controversy intensified after Owens appeared on a discussion panel alongside Russian officials who have been sanctioned by the United States and other Western governments. Among those present were Alexander Zharov, a Russian media executive appointed by President Vladimir Putin, and Anna Kuznetsova, a member of Russia's parliament who has been sanctioned over allegations related to the deportation of Ukrainian children.
Several conservative commentators and activists publicly condemned Owens. Radio host Mark Levin sharply criticized her on social media, while activist Laura Loomer called for an investigation into whether Owens' activities should be examined under the Foreign Agents Registration Act. The conservative podcast Chick

item.title: Michelle Phillips’ Career Reinvention in the 1970s: Acting, Music, and Hollywood Fame
item.description:
The article highlights Michelle Phillips’ transformation during the 1970s after the final breakup of The Mamas & the Papas in 1971. Rather than remaining defined by her success in the influential 1960s folk-rock group, Phillips embarked on a new chapter focused primarily on acting. Supported by royalties from her music career, she studied acting in Los Angeles and gradually built a reputation as a serious performer in Hollywood.
Throughout the decade, she appeared in several notable films, including 'The Last Movie' (1971), 'Dillinger' (1973), 'Valentino' (1977), and 'Bloodline' (1979). These roles helped establish her as a versatile actress and demonstrated her ability to succeed outside the music industry. Although acting became her primary profession, Phillips briefly returned to recording and released her only solo studio album, 'Victim of Romance', in 1977. Produced by acclaimed musician and producer Jack Nitzsche, the album featured a theatrical retro-pop style that differed sig

item.title: Mike Davis Criticizes Senate Majority Leader John Thune During Steve Bannon Podcast Appearance
item.description:
Mike Davis, a Trump ally and founder of the Article III Project, sharply criticized Senate Majority Leader John Thune during an appearance on Steve Bannon's War Room podcast. Davis used profane language while reacting to comments Thune made regarding the potential Senate confirmation process for Todd Blanche, who was recently nominated by President Donald Trump to serve permanently as Attorney General after previously acting in the role. Davis argued that Thune's cautious public remarks demonstrated weak leadership and repeated personal insults directed at the South Dakota senator.
The discussion also covered Trump's recent appointment of Bill Pulte as acting Director of National Intelligence. Some Republican lawmakers expressed concerns about Pulte's lack of intelligence experience, but Davis defended the selection. He argued that appointing someone from outside the intelligence establishment could help prevent the politicization of intelligence agencies, citing former intelligence o

item.title: Fan arrested and permanently banned after running onto NBA Finals court to take selfie with Victor Wembanyama
item.description: During Game 1 of the NBA Finals between the New York Knicks and the San Antonio Spurs, a fan disrupted play by running onto the court in the fourth quarter in an attempt to take a selfie video with Spurs star Victor Wembanyama. The incident occurred while the Knicks were leading and briefly interrupted the flow of the game. Security personnel quickly responded, detaining the individual and escorting him off the court without any reported contact with players. An NBA spokesperson later confirmed that the fan was arrested and issued a lifetime ban from all NBA arenas. A second individual involved in the incident also received a lifetime ban. Wembanyama later said he was surprised by the situation and unsure how to react, noting it was a bizarre moment during a high-stakes game. Despite the disruption, the game continued, and the New York Knicks defeated the Spurs 105-95, extending their playoff winning streak to 12 games, a notable achievement in NBA postseason history. Coaches and offic

item.title: Internet Reacts to AI-Generated Image of Trump in Football Attire Shared During Pride Month
item.description: President Donald Trump has once again sparked widespread online discussion after an AI-generated image featuring him circulated on social media. The image depicts Trump wearing a No. 47 football jersey paired with tight American flag shorts, holding a basketball, and surrounded by male cheerleaders. Although the image no longer appears on his Truth Social account, reactions poured in from various public figures and commentators, especially given the timing during Pride Month in June. Critics and humorists seized on the visuals, with some sarcastically linking it to Pride Month celebrations. Retired USAF Colonel Moe Davis commented on what he saw as an endorsement of Pride Month. Journalist John Harwood simply responded with 'Hmmm,' while former Republican strategist Rick Wilson posted the image alongside a rainbow flag emoji and the words 'Happy Pride!' Gay rights advocate Martina Navratilova jokingly questioned if Trump was gay, and the hacktivist group Anonymous called it 'peak MAGA.

item.title: Researchers Explore Links Between Gum Disease, Oral Bacteria, and Cognitive Decline
item.description: Researchers are increasingly investigating whether oral health, particularly gum disease, may be connected to brain health and cognitive decline. Although there is currently no proof that gum disease directly causes Alzheimer’s disease, scientific studies suggest that chronic oral infections and inflammation could play a role in processes associated with dementia. Gum disease is a long-term inflammatory condition caused by bacterial buildup and is commonly characterized by symptoms such as bleeding gums, bad breath, swelling, and loose teeth. Because it often develops without significant pain, many individuals do not seek treatment until substantial damage has occurred. One bacterium of particular interest is Porphyromonas gingivalis, which is strongly associated with severe gum disease. Researchers have identified evidence of this bacterium and its toxic enzymes in the brains of people diagnosed with Alzheimer’s disease, raising questions about possible biological connections between

item.title: Todd Blanche's Friendship with Ghislaine Maxwell's Attorney Raises Concerns During Attorney General Confirmation Process
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, President Donald Trump's former personal attorney and current nominee for the top Justice Department position, faces potential scrutiny during his Senate confirmation hearings. According to a report, senators may question Blanche about his relationship with David Oscar Markus, the defense attorney representing Ghislaine Maxwell, who was convicted in connection with sex trafficking alongside Jeffrey Epstein. Markus previously appeared on a podcast with Blanche in 2024, where Blanche described him warmly as a friend and one of the best in the field. This connection has raised alarms among survivors of Epstein and Maxwell's victims, especially as Maxwell has expressed interest in a potential pardon from Trump and agreed to testify before the House Oversight Committee under certain conditions. The article highlights concerns that Blanche's professional ties could influence decisions regarding clemency for Maxwell. Critics question whether such relation

item.title: John Bolton Reaches Plea Deal on Classified Documents Charges
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton has reached a plea agreement with federal prosecutors and is set to plead guilty to one count of illegal retention of sensitive national security documents. According to sources cited by CNN, Bolton will also pay a fine exceeding $2 million. The hearing is scheduled for June 26 in U.S. District Court. The case stems from allegations that Bolton kept diary-like entries from his time in the Trump White House and shared over 1,000 pages of classified notes with his wife and daughter through a personal email account. He was originally indicted on 18 counts, including eight for illegal transmission of national defense information and 10 for illegal retention. The transmission charges are not included in the plea deal. A conviction on the retention count carries a possible sentence of zero to 60 months in prison. This prosecution differs from other high-profile cases involving figures like James Comey and Letitia James, as Bolton's case maintained

item.title: Leaked communications suggest King Charles had concerns about hosting Trump's 2025 UK state visit due to Ukraine-related tensions
item.description: A report by The i Paper, cited by Raw Story, alleges that King Charles III privately expressed reservations about hosting U.S. President Donald Trump during a state visit to the United Kingdom in 2025. According to leaked emails, text messages, and other communications contained in what has been described as the 'Mandelson files,' the monarch was concerned about Trump's treatment of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky during a contentious White House meeting in February 2025. Sources quoted in the report claimed that King Charles was uneasy about the visit, with one source describing him as 'jittery' and another stating that he 'did not want to do it.' Officials reportedly feared that any perceived royal reluctance could create a diplomatic dispute between the United Kingdom and the United States. The leaked communications indicate that British diplomats and government officials worked behind the scenes to address the King's concerns and ensure the visit proceeded smoothly. Messages

item.title: Tech-Backed Candidates Suffer Major Defeats in 2026 Primary Elections
item.description:
A report highlighted the poor performance of several candidates backed by wealthy technology executives and investors during the 2026 U.S. primary elections. Despite receiving substantial financial support from Silicon Valley donors, a number of candidates advocating policies favorable to the technology sector failed to gain voter support. One of the most notable losses was San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan, a former startup executive who raised tens of millions of dollars through his connections in the technology industry but quickly conceded defeat in California's gubernatorial race after receiving only a small share of the vote.
The trend extended to congressional contests as well. Entrepreneur Ethan Agarwal lost decisively to Representative Ro Khanna, who has supported proposals to increase taxes on California billionaires. Venture capitalist Eric Jones also finished well behind incumbent Representative Mike Thompson in Napa County. Political strategists cited these outcomes as evidence t

item.title: Analysis Suggests Amy Coney Barrett May Support Broad Limits on Federal Court Challenges to Executive Actions
item.description: A Raw Story report highlights an analysis by Georgetown University law professor Steve Vladeck regarding a recent Supreme Court case, Margolin v. National Association of Immigration Judges. Although the Court's May 26 decision focused on a narrow procedural issue, Vladeck argues that a separate concurrence written by Justice Clarence Thomas and joined by Justice Amy Coney Barrett may provide insight into Barrett's future approach to major legal disputes involving the Trump administration. The concurrence supported a stronger interpretation of the legal doctrine known as 'channeling,' which requires plaintiffs to pursue claims through designated administrative processes before seeking relief in federal court. According to the analysis, the Trump administration has relied heavily on this doctrine in several ongoing disputes, including immigration and federal grant-related cases. Critics argue that some of the administrative forums involved are controlled by the executive branch, potentia

item.title: Nick Anderson Editorial Cartoon Examines MAGA Politics Through a UFC-Inspired Metaphor
item.description: This Raw Story piece is not a traditional news report but an opinion feature centered on an editorial cartoon by Pulitzer Prize-winning cartoonist Nick Anderson. Published in Raw Story's opinion section, the work uses the imagery and symbolism of a cage fight—commonly associated with UFC events—to comment on contemporary MAGA politics and the political climate surrounding former President Donald Trump and his supporters. The article itself provides very little accompanying text and primarily serves as a vehicle for presenting Anderson's cartoon and viewpoint. The headline, 'The real cage fight,' suggests that the cartoon draws a comparison between combat sports and ongoing political battles within the Republican movement, the MAGA base, or the broader U.S. political arena. Rather than reporting new facts or developments, the feature offers political commentary through visual satire, a format that allows the artist to critique public figures, political strategies, and partisan conflicts

item.title: Escalating Geopolitical Tensions in the Baltic Sea Region Between NATO and Russia
item.description: The Baltic Sea region is currently viewed by security experts as one of the highest-risk areas for potential direct conflict between NATO and Russia. The article highlights how NATO's eastward expansion has transformed the Baltic Sea into what is effectively a NATO-controlled body of water, with the alliance controlling approximately 92% of the coastline. Russia's access remains limited to small stretches near Kaliningrad and St. Petersburg. Key flashpoints include the strategic vulnerability of St. Petersburg at the end of the Gulf of Finland, surrounded by NATO members Finland and Estonia, and the Russian enclave of Kaliningrad, which is geographically isolated within NATO territory. The Suwalki Gap between Poland and Lithuania represents another critical chokepoint that could lead to rapid escalation in a crisis. The author notes provocative actions such as Ukrainian drones using Baltic airspace for strikes on Russian targets, raising concerns about neutrality violations. Statements

item.title: Legal Analyst Says Revised DOJ Indictment Against SPLC Still Fails to Address Core Fraud Allegations
item.description: A new superseding indictment filed by the U.S. Department of Justice against the Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC) has drawn criticism from national security journalist Marcy Wheeler, who argues that the revised filing fails to resolve fundamental weaknesses in the government's case. According to Wheeler, the DOJ attempted to correct deficiencies in its original indictment by shifting the focus of the allegations. The case centers on claims that SPLC misled donors regarding how donated funds were used, particularly in connection with the organization's use of undercover informants to infiltrate and monitor extremist groups such as the Ku Klux Klan. In the original indictment, prosecutors allegedly suggested that SPLC falsely represented how donor money would be spent. However, Wheeler notes that neither the original nor the revised indictment provides evidence that SPLC explicitly promised donors that informants would not be used. The new filing instead emphasizes alleged omissions of

item.title: Conservative Figures Clash Over Candace Owens' Participation in Russian Economic Forum
item.description:
Conservative commentator Candace Owens has become the center of a dispute within right-wing political circles after appearing at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum in Russia. Owens attended a panel discussion alongside Russian officials, including Alexander Zharov, a Kremlin-appointed executive who is under U.S. sanctions, and Anna Kuznetsova, a Russian lawmaker sanctioned by both the United States and the European Union. Her appearance prompted strong criticism from several prominent conservative personalities.
Radio host Mark Levin publicly condemned Owens, while activist Laura Loomer called for an investigation into whether her activities should be examined under the Foreign Agents Registration Act. Loomer also made personal criticisms of Owens and her family. Additional criticism came from the conservative media personalities behind Chicks on the Right, who argued that Owens' participation benefited Russian interests and questioned the circumstances surrounding her tr

item.title: US House Approves Resolution Seeking Congressional Oversight of Military Action Against Iran
item.description:
The United States House of Representatives approved a war powers resolution intended to limit President Donald Trump’s ability to continue military operations against Iran without explicit authorization from Congress. The measure passed by a narrow 215-208 margin, with four Republican representatives joining Democrats in support. The vote represents a significant challenge to the administration’s handling of the conflict and reflects growing concern among lawmakers about the constitutional balance of war-making powers.
The resolution follows Trump's decision to join Israel in military operations against Iran on February 28 without seeking prior congressional approval. Supporters of the measure argue that the Constitution grants Congress the authority to declare war and that any extended military engagement should require legislative authorization. Several lawmakers emphasized that the time limits provided under the War Powers Act have expired and that Congress should determine the fut

item.title: Ohio Senate Republicans Advance Voter ID Constitutional Amendment Despite Internal Party Criticism
item.description: Ohio Senate Republicans successfully passed Senate Joint Resolution 10 on Wednesday, aiming to enshrine a photo voter ID requirement into the state constitution. The measure passed by a 22-9 vote, moving quickly through the chamber just weeks after its introduction. While photo ID has been mandatory at polling places since 2023, GOP leaders believe constitutional protection is necessary to prevent future changes. However, the resolution has sparked significant internal divisions among conservatives. The proposal notably omits stricter rules for mail-in ballots, which continue under more lenient identification standards. A committee tweak further deferred some details to future legislators, drawing sharp criticism from within the party. Senator Al Cutrona, a Republican from Canfield, announced he voted against the measure in its current form and expressed hope that the House would address the shortcomings. State Representative Ron Ferguson also voiced opposition, arguing the amendment w

item.title: Rare Photos Highlight Angelina Jolie’s Teenage Years and Early Path to Stardom
item.description:
Vintage Everyday published a collection of candid photographs showing Angelina Jolie during her teenage years in the late 1980s and early 1990s, offering a look at the period before she became a globally recognized actress and humanitarian. Raised in Los Angeles as the daughter of actor Jon Voight and Marcheline Bertrand, Jolie began working as a model at age 14. Early photo shoots by photographers such as Harry Langdon and Sean McCall showcased the distinctive facial features that would later contribute to her fame, including her prominent lips and high cheekbones.
The article describes how Jolie struggled to fit in during her time at Beverly Hills High School and later Moreno High School. Feeling disconnected from many of her peers, she embraced punk and goth influences, often wearing black clothing, experimenting with unconventional hair colors, and developing interests that reflected her rebellious personality. During this period, she reportedly faced frustrations with acting oppo

item.title: Joyce Vance Warns Democrats to Increase Voter Mobilization After Supreme Court Decision on Alabama Redistricting
item.description:
The article discusses a reaction from former federal prosecutor Joyce Vance to a recent U.S. Supreme Court decision that allowed Alabama to use a congressional map despite lower federal court findings that the map was intentionally racially discriminatory. Writing in her Civil Discourse newsletter, Vance argued that the ruling represents the latest step in a long-term weakening of protections for Black voters. She linked the decision to a series of major court actions over the past decade, including the 2013 Shelby County v. Holder ruling and more recent decisions affecting enforcement of the Voting Rights Act.
According to Vance, the Supreme Court relied on the Purcell principle, a doctrine that generally discourages courts from changing election rules close to an election. She criticized the application of that doctrine in this case, arguing that it effectively altered the electoral landscape by permitting maps that had already been found problematic by a lower court.
The article h

item.title: House Vote Limiting Trump's Iran War Powers Signals Growing Republican Dissent
item.description:
A resolution passed by the U.S. House of Representatives has emerged as one of the most significant congressional rebukes of President Donald Trump's administration. The measure, approved by a 215-208 vote with four Republicans joining Democrats, seeks to restrict the president's authority to conduct military operations in Iran without explicit authorization from Congress. If the Senate also approves the resolution, Trump would be required either to withdraw U.S. forces from Iran or obtain congressional approval for continued military action.
Political analyst Aaron Blake argued that the vote reflects increasing frustration among some Republicans over Trump's handling of the Iran situation and the political consequences associated with it. According to the analysis, the outcome suggests that a growing minority within the Republican Party is becoming less willing to automatically support the president.
The article also highlights several additional challenges facing the administration

item.title: Understanding Stochastic Gradient Descent in PyTorch Through a Linear Regression Example
item.description:
This article explains how PyTorch uses Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) to train machine learning models by minimizing prediction error. The author introduces a simple linear regression model defined by the equation y = wx + b and demonstrates how the model learns the optimal weight and bias values through iterative updates. The discussion begins with the Mean Squared Error (MSE) loss function, which measures the difference between predicted and actual outputs. During training, the model calculates gradients of the loss with respect to the parameters and updates them using a learning rate.
The article provides a detailed explanation of forward propagation, where predictions and loss values are computed, and backward propagation, where gradients are calculated using the chain rule. To illustrate the process, the author breaks down the computational graph used by PyTorch's automatic differentiation system and derives the mathematical formulas for the gradients of both the weight and bi

item.title: Former Trump Administration Staffer Says Kaitlan Collins Was One of the Most Challenging Reporters for White House Officials
item.description:
A former White House staff member from Donald Trump's first administration said that CNN journalist Kaitlan Collins was regarded as one of the most difficult reporters for the administration to handle during press briefings. Speaking on CNN's 'NewsNight,' former deputy press secretary Sarah Matthews commented on a recent exchange between Trump and Collins in which the president criticized the reporter after she questioned him about a fund established to compensate individuals involved in the January 6 events and other political allies.
According to Matthews, Collins' persistence and effectiveness as a journalist made both Trump and former White House press secretary Kayleigh McEnany uncomfortable during press conferences. She claimed there were occasions when Collins was intentionally not called upon because officials preferred to avoid answering her questions. Matthews praised Collins' reporting skills and argued that Trump's repeated criticism of her stems from her ability to ask ch

item.title: Trump Criticizes Four House Republicans After War Powers Resolution on Iran Passes
item.description:
President Donald Trump strongly criticized four Republican members of the U.S. House of Representatives after they joined Democrats in supporting a war powers resolution related to U.S. involvement with Iran. The measure passed the Republican-controlled House by a vote of 215-208, with Representatives Tom Barrett of Michigan, Warren Davidson of Ohio, Brian Fitzpatrick of Pennsylvania, and Thomas Massie of Kentucky voting alongside the Democratic minority.
Following the vote, Trump posted comments on Truth Social condemning the lawmakers and describing them as 'grandstanders.' He argued that the resolution came at a sensitive moment while his administration was engaged in negotiations aimed at ending the conflict with the Islamic Republic of Iran. Trump also accused Democrats of opposing him for political reasons and claimed they would rather undermine his administration than allow him to achieve another policy victory.
The resolution seeks to limit the president's ability to continue

item.title: Scott Pelley Departs ‘60 Minutes’ Following Dispute With Executive Producer Nick Bilton
item.description: Veteran journalist and longtime ‘60 Minutes’ correspondent Scott Pelley was dismissed from the CBS newsmagazine program following a confrontation with the show's newly appointed executive producer, Nick Bilton. According to the article, the firing occurred on Tuesday, one day after a heated dispute between Pelley and Bilton. Sources familiar with the situation indicated that the disagreement escalated significantly, ultimately leading to the decision to terminate Pelley's role on the program. Bilton informed staff members through an internal email that ‘60 Minutes’ had ‘parted ways’ with Pelley, signaling the end of his association with one of the most recognizable television news programs in the United States. The development represents another major change during a period of instability and organizational upheaval for the iconic newsmagazine. While the article excerpt does not provide detailed information regarding the specific subject of the confrontation, the timing suggests a dire

item.title: Staff Departures and Editorial Disputes Raise Concerns About the Future of CBS's 60 Minutes
item.description:
A report highlighted growing turmoil at CBS News and its potential impact on the long-running television news program '60 Minutes.' The controversy follows major staffing changes that reportedly reduced the number of correspondents on the show from seven to four. According to accounts cited in the article, correspondent Scott Pelley criticized CBS leadership during a staff meeting after the dismissal of fellow correspondents Sharyn Alfonsi and Cecilia Vega. Pelley allegedly questioned the qualifications of new leadership and accused management of damaging the program's legacy.
The dispute extends beyond personnel decisions. Several current and former correspondents have reportedly accused CBS management of interfering with editorial independence and introducing bias to accommodate the Trump administration, allegations that CBS has denied. The situation has intensified concerns among media observers because '60 Minutes' remains one of the most successful programs in network television

item.title: Trump Alleges Election Irregularities in California Primary Vote Count During Early-Morning Posts
item.description:
President Donald Trump posted a series of messages on Truth Social early Thursday morning criticizing the pace of vote counting in California's primary elections. In posts published shortly after 1 a.m., Trump alleged that California election officials and Democrats were attempting to influence the outcomes of the Republican primary contests for governor and mayor of Los Angeles. He specifically referenced former Fox News host Steve Hilton, who is running for governor, and reality television personality Spencer Pratt, who is seeking the office of Los Angeles mayor.
Trump argued that the continued counting of mail-in ballots was evidence of potential election misconduct. He questioned why vote totals were not finalized and claimed that large numbers of mail ballots were arriving after Election Day. California law, however, permits election officials to count mail-in ballots that are postmarked on or before Election Day and received within seven days afterward, which can delay the relea

item.title: A Look Back at Gloria Paul’s Career and Legacy in Italian Popular Cinema
item.description:
The article celebrates the life and career of Gloria Paul, a British-born actress, singer, and dancer who became a recognizable figure in Italian popular cinema during the 1960s and 1970s. Known for her striking appearance, stage presence, and comedic talent, Paul first gained attention as a dancer in London's West End and at the famous Lido in Paris. Her move to Rome during the height of the Dolce Vita era opened the door to a successful film career in Italy.
Paul became a familiar face in a variety of genres, including musical comedies, parody westerns, and cult comedy films. She worked alongside some of Italy's most celebrated comedic performers, including Franco and Ciccio as well as Totò. Her performances helped establish her reputation as a versatile entertainer capable of combining glamour with humor.
Beyond her contributions to cinema, the article highlights Paul's personal resilience. In the late 1990s, she suffered a tragic domestic accident that left her paralyzed. Despite

item.title: Opinion Column Criticizes CBS Leadership Changes and Defends Scott Pelley's Journalistic Independence
item.description: This opinion article by Tara Dublin argues that the controversy surrounding veteran journalist Scott Pelley is part of a broader transformation within CBS News and its flagship program, 60 Minutes. The author begins with personal memories of watching 60 Minutes as a trusted source of journalism and describes the program's long-standing reputation for investigative reporting and factual accuracy. The article contends that recent corporate and editorial changes at CBS and Paramount have undermined those traditions. Dublin criticizes the network's handling of political coverage, including disputes involving Donald Trump, and claims that new leadership has prioritized political considerations over journalistic standards. The piece specifically targets Bari Weiss and Nick Bilton, portraying them as inexperienced choices for leadership roles within the news organization. According to the article, Bilton was responsible for Pelley's dismissal and that decision allegedly stemmed from Pelley's

item.title: Angrite Meteorite NWA 12774 Reveals Evidence of a Large Protoplanet in the Early Solar System
item.description: A recent study published in Earth and Planetary Science Letters presents compelling evidence of a massive protoplanet that existed in the early solar system approximately 4.5 billion years ago. Researchers from the University of Colorado Boulder analyzed the Northwest Africa 12774 angrite meteorite, discovered in the Sahara Desert. Angrites are rare volcanic rocks dating back to the solar system's formation, with only 68 known specimens among over 80,000 meteorites found on Earth. This particular meteorite contains clinopyroxene crystals unusually rich in aluminum, indicating formation under extreme pressures exceeding 17.5 kilobars. Such conditions are impossible within small asteroids, suggesting the parent body had a radius of at least 1,000 kilometers. Further analysis of crystal structures points to an even larger size, potentially up to 1,800 kilometers in radius, comparable to the Moon or approaching Mars' dimensions. This challenges prior assumptions that angrites originated so

item.title: Five Cruise Ship Passengers Leave Nebraska Hantavirus Monitoring Facility and Continue Observation in Their Home States
item.description:
Five passengers who were being monitored at a federal quarantine facility in Nebraska after potential exposure to the Andes strain of hantavirus have left the facility and returned to their home states. According to Nebraska Medicine, these individuals were among 18 passengers from a cruise ship where possible exposure to the virus had occurred. Health officials had strongly encouraged all potentially exposed passengers to remain at the Nebraska quarantine unit for the full recommended 42-day monitoring period because the Andes strain is associated with a prolonged incubation period and can pose serious health risks.
However, some passengers chose to leave after approximately three weeks of observation. Nebraska Medicine stated that these individuals will continue to be monitored for the remaining 21 days by local and state public health authorities in their respective home states. The transition of monitoring responsibilities is intended to ensure that any symptoms potentially relate

item.title: Google Commits to Replenishing 120% of Data Center Water Consumption by 2030
item.description: Google has announced a series of new water stewardship commitments aimed at addressing concerns about the environmental impact of its data centers, particularly as artificial intelligence infrastructure expands. The company acknowledged growing public scrutiny over the large amounts of water required to cool servers and operate AI systems, while maintaining that its overall contribution to U.S. water consumption remains relatively small. As part of its updated strategy, Google pledged to replenish more water than it consumes at its data center sites by 2030, targeting a replenishment rate of 120% of usage. The company reported that it replenished more than 7 billion gallons of water in 2025 and currently operates 165 water stewardship projects across 97 watersheds. Once fully implemented, these projects are expected to replenish more than 19 billion gallons annually by 2030. Google also stated that it has committed over $500 million to support water, wastewater, and water reuse infrast

item.title: Political commentator urges Trump to consider stimulus payments to offset tariff-related economic pressure on working-class voters
item.description:
A political debate on CNN's 'NewsNight' sparked attention after commentator Batya Ungar-Sargon, who describes herself as a 'MAGA lefty,' argued that President Donald Trump should consider issuing new stimulus checks to Americans affected by the economic consequences of his policies. During the discussion, Ungar-Sargon claimed that many working-class voters who supported Trump are struggling with higher living costs, particularly for necessities such as food and fuel. She argued that these voters are disproportionately bearing the burden of economic disruptions associated with Trump's tariff policies and broader international tensions.
Ungar-Sargon suggested that direct financial assistance, which she described as a tariff rebate or stimulus payment, could help alleviate the economic hardship facing these households. Her proposal drew immediate reactions from other panelists. Political analyst Bakari Sellers expressed surprise at the recommendation, while host Abby Phillip questioned w

item.title: Mick Foley Explains Why He Chose Not to Renew His WWE Legends Contract Over the Company's Ties to Donald Trump
item.description: WWE Hall of Famer Mick Foley explained in an interview with Ariel Helwani why he decided to distance himself from WWE and allow his Legends contract to expire. Foley, known for wrestling personas such as Mankind, Cactus Jack, and Dude Love, said his decision was driven by his opposition to President Donald Trump and by WWE's longstanding relationship with him. According to Foley, the decisive moment came after comments Trump made following the deaths of filmmaker Rob Reiner and his wife, Michele Reiner. Foley described those remarks as cruel and insensitive, saying they represented the final incident that convinced him he could no longer remain associated with the company while maintaining his personal principles. Although Foley emphasized that he still loves WWE and does not wish to criticize the organization itself, he felt remaining silent would make him complicit. He noted that his name has been linked to WWE for more than three decades and that he held a Legends agreement despite

item.title: Trump Declines to Confirm Cancellation of Controversial Compensation Fund, Creating Uncertainty Among Republicans
item.description:
President Donald Trump sparked renewed concern among congressional Republicans after declining to confirm whether his administration would abandon a controversial $1.776 billion compensation fund. The proposed fund was expected to be created through a settlement related to a lawsuit Trump filed against the Internal Revenue Service in 2019 concerning the disclosure of his tax returns. The plan drew criticism because several Trump allies indicated they might seek payments from the fund, raising questions about its purpose and beneficiaries.
The controversy intensified after Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche told Congress that the administration had decided not to proceed with the fund. Republican lawmakers appeared relieved by those statements, believing the issue had been resolved and that political fallout could be avoided. However, Trump's comments during a Wednesday press interaction created fresh uncertainty. When asked directly whether the plan had been abandoned, he did not pr

item.title: House Ethics Committee Probes Possible Leaks Related to Dismissed Jim Costa Investigation
item.description:
The House Ethics Committee is reportedly conducting an investigation into possible leaks of confidential information connected to a previous ethics case involving Representative Jim Costa (D-CA). According to a report by NOTUS, the committee has sent letters to former members who participated in deliberations regarding allegations made against Costa in 2023. Those allegations accused the congressman of making inappropriate advances toward staff members. The case was ultimately dismissed, but details from the committee's internal discussions were later disclosed to the media.
The report states that individuals who were present during the committee's deliberations are now being asked whether they shared confidential information outside the committee. Responses are reportedly required to be submitted under oath, indicating the seriousness of the inquiry. Sources cited by NOTUS said that some committee members had previously expressed concerns about Costa's behavior during those discussio

item.title: Sen. Ron Johnson Supports Compensation for Alleged Government Abuse Victims but Opposes Future Immunity Provisions
item.description:
A debate has emerged among Republicans over proposals associated with President Donald Trump’s effort to compensate individuals who claim they were harmed by federal investigations and prosecutions related to January 6 and other political matters. Although Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche told Congress that a proposed $1.8 billion compensation fund would not move forward, Trump later indicated that he still supports the idea and has not ruled it out.
Representative Barry Loudermilk of Georgia, who was scrutinized by the House January 6 Committee, said false accusations had a significant impact on him and his family, including the need for security measures. While he opposes payments to individuals who assaulted law enforcement officers during the Capitol riot, he suggested that compensation for others who were prosecuted could be considered.
Senator Ron Johnson of Wisconsin also expressed support for allowing citizens who were allegedly harmed by government actions to seek compen

item.title: SPLC Asks Court to Investigate Whether DOJ Improperly Released Superseding Indictment to the Media
item.description:
A legal dispute has intensified between the U.S. Department of Justice and the Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC), with the organization accusing federal officials of violating grand jury secrecy rules. According to a court filing submitted by SPLC attorneys, Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche and members of the DOJ allegedly distributed a copy of a superseding indictment to members of the press before it had been officially entered into the court docket. The SPLC argues that such an action conflicts with rules designed to protect the confidentiality of grand jury proceedings and related court documents prior to their formal release.
In its filing, the organization criticized what it described as a pattern of improper public communications by DOJ officials regarding the case. Attorneys referenced earlier incidents in which Blanche allegedly made inaccurate public statements about the investigation, prompting corrections and judicial concerns about the government's duty of candor. Th

item.title: How AI-Native Specifications Reduce Errors in AI Coding Agents
item.description:
The article discusses a recurring problem in modern software development: AI coding agents often produce code that unintentionally breaks existing systems, even when given clear requirements. The author argues that the root cause is not poor AI performance but rather the mismatch between human-written specifications and machine interpretation. Traditional specification standards such as IEEE 830 and ISO/IEC 29148 were designed for human readers, who naturally resolve ambiguity using context, experience, and clarification. AI agents, however, interpret instructions literally and fill in missing details based on training data, which can lead to unintended consequences.
To address this issue, the author introduces ANSS (AI-Native System Specification Standard), a structured approach designed specifically for AI agents as primary readers of technical specifications. ANSS introduces several key mechanisms. First, a three-layer markup system separates domain intent, engineering implementati

item.title: Fox News Poll Shows Democratic Leads in Key Ohio Senate and Governor Races
item.description: A new Fox News poll suggests that Republicans may face significant challenges in several high-profile Ohio races ahead of the upcoming elections. The survey found a notable decline in President Donald Trump’s standing among Ohio voters. According to the poll, 42% of respondents view Trump favorably while 57% view him unfavorably, representing a major shift from the positive net rating he received in a Fox News voter survey conducted during the 2024 election cycle. The poll also shows Democrats performing strongly in two major statewide contests. Former U.S. Senator Sherrod Brown, who lost his reelection bid in 2024, is now leading Republican Senator Jon Husted by eight percentage points, 53% to 45%. In the gubernatorial race, former Ohio public health official Dr. Amy Acton holds a narrow one-point advantage over Republican businessman Vivek Ramaswamy, leading 50% to 49%. These results are noteworthy because Ohio has generally leaned Republican in recent decades. Democrats have not won

item.title: Report Says Trump Plans to Nominate Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche for Permanent Attorney General Role
item.description:
According to a report cited by CNN, President Donald Trump is expected to formally nominate Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to serve as the nation's attorney general, potentially as early as Wednesday evening. Blanche has been serving in the acting role since former Attorney General Pam Bondi was removed from the position in April. Prior to joining the Department of Justice, Blanche was widely known for serving as one of Trump's defense attorneys in several legal matters.
During his tenure as acting attorney general, Blanche has overseen a number of significant and controversial initiatives. Among them was an effort to establish a $1.776 billion compensation fund intended for individuals who claimed they had been wrongfully prosecuted by the federal government. The proposal attracted public attention and debate regarding its scope and cost.
The reported nomination comes shortly after Blanche appeared before the House Appropriations Committee during an oversight hearing focused o

item.title: Lauren Boebert criticizes lack of transparency over GOP lawmaker Tom Kean Jr.'s prolonged absence
item.description:
Republican Congresswoman Lauren Boebert has publicly expressed frustration over the prolonged and unexplained absence of fellow Republican Rep. Tom Kean Jr. of New Jersey. According to reports, Kean has been away from congressional duties for more than two months due to undisclosed medical issues. His absence has reportedly caused growing concern within Republican leadership, as details about his condition, recovery process, and expected return timeline have not been clearly communicated.
Despite being physically absent, Kean has recently begun filing legislation and has provided limited updates regarding his situation. In a statement cited by political media outlets, he indicated that he is currently focused on recovery under medical guidance and expects to transition from virtual participation back to in-person work within weeks. He also stated that he plans to be fully transparent about the nature of his medical condition once he resumes regular duties.
Boebert voiced her frustrat

item.title: A Retrospective of Henri Gray's Belle Époque Posters and Contributions to French Graphic Art
item.description:
The article highlights the life and artistic legacy of Henri Gray (1858–1924), a French illustrator and poster artist born Henri Boulanger who worked during the Belle Époque, a period known for its flourishing arts and culture in France. Gray became recognized for creating sophisticated advertising posters that combined artistic elegance with commercial effectiveness. His style incorporated flowing lines, balanced compositions, and influences from Art Nouveau while maintaining strong illustrative traditions. Many of his works featured graceful female figures, theatrical themes, entertainment venues, and promotional imagery for products and events.
The feature presents a broad selection of Gray’s poster designs produced between the late 1880s and the early 1900s. These include advertisements for circuses, theaters, concerts, bicycle exhibitions, railways, seaside destinations, automobiles, petroleum products, and commercial businesses. Among the showcased works are posters for Cirque d

item.title: Andrew Berry Praises Jared Verse as Key Piece of Browns’ Return in Myles Garrett Trade
item.description:
Cleveland Browns General Manager Andrew Berry emphasized that newly acquired edge rusher Jared Verse was the most important component of the team's return package in the blockbuster trade that sent Myles Garrett to the Los Angeles Rams. Speaking to reporters, Berry explained that while Garrett was the centerpiece of the overall transaction, the inclusion of Verse was what made the deal attractive and realistic for Cleveland.
Berry described Verse as an outstanding young player and an ideal fit for the Browns' aggressive defensive scheme. He highlighted Verse's ability to impact games both as a pass rusher and run defender, noting that his style and skill set align perfectly with the identity Cleveland has built on defense over recent seasons. Berry also stressed that acquiring a proven player like Verse allows the Browns to remain competitive defensively rather than sacrificing present performance solely for future assets.
Verse, who was selected 19th overall in the 2024 NFL Draft, h

item.title: Rep. Randy Fine suggests banning commentator Hasan Piker from the United States after UK entry denial
item.description:
Republican Rep. Randy Fine of Florida has sparked controversy after suggesting that progressive political commentator Hasan Piker should be banned from entering the United States. The comments came after Piker, a U.S. citizen and prominent online streamer known for his political commentary, was reportedly denied entry to the United Kingdom alongside commentator Cenk Uygur. According to reporting cited in the article, the pair were scheduled to speak at a SXSW London Festival event but were turned away, with some reports linking the decision to their criticism of Israel.
When asked by a TMZ reporter about Piker’s situation, Fine argued that Piker should not be allowed into the United States either. He framed his stance in terms of national security and free speech, claiming that while freedom of expression exists, individuals he characterized as supporters of terrorism should be held accountable. Fine made additional claims about Piker’s background and alleged political influence, call

item.title: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna alleges activist made physical contact during confrontation at event
item.description:
Representative Anna Paulina Luna, a Republican from Florida and ally of President Donald Trump, has alleged that an anti-war activist physically struck her during a recent confrontation. The incident reportedly involved members associated with the activist group Code Pink and occurred while Luna was engaging with attendees and later attempting to leave the area. According to statements provided by Luna and cited in reporting, the activist approached her while questioning her about her participation in a congressional hearing involving Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Luna claims that as she tried to walk away from the confrontation, the individual made physical contact with her arm. She later described the interaction in a video posted to her social media account, asserting that public officials should not be subjected to physical contact or harm during political disagreements.
Video footage obtained by TMZ appears to show a verbal exchange between Luna and the activist, followed by a

item.title: Four House Republicans join Democrats in vote to limit Trump’s war powers, CNN reports
item.description:
A recent House of Representatives vote has raised concerns within the White House after four Republican lawmakers broke ranks with their party to support a measure aimed at limiting President Donald Trump’s war powers, according to CNN reporting. The vote, which seeks to curb the president’s authority over military action, marks a notable instance of Republican dissent on a key issue tied to the administration’s foreign policy. Although the measure still requires approval from the Senate before it can take effect, the development has been interpreted as a warning sign for the White House.
CNN correspondent Kristen Holmes reported that several officials within the Trump administration expressed significant concern following the vote, describing an atmosphere of unease about the president’s political standing. Holmes highlighted that the bipartisan split was particularly striking, noting that four Republicans voted alongside Democrats, a scenario she suggested would have been unlikely j

item.title: BuzzFeed Publishes Personality Quiz Matching Readers With a Backrooms Character
item.description:
BuzzFeed published an interactive entertainment quiz created by Terry Carter Jr. that invites readers to discover which character from the Backrooms universe they most resemble. The quiz is designed around a series of personality-based questions and promises to match participants with a specific Backrooms character based on their answers. The article briefly introduces the Backrooms as a strange, unpredictable environment populated by individuals attempting to understand and survive impossible situations. It suggests that readers may relate to the personalities and behaviors of these characters and encourages them to explore those similarities through the quiz.
The post includes a note warning readers about mild visual spoilers, indicating that some images or references may reveal small details from the Backrooms franchise. Rather than providing news, analysis, or detailed information about the characters, the content primarily serves as a piece of interactive entertainment intended f

item.title: Robert Reich Argues Trump Is Using Voter Fraud Claims to Justify Election Restrictions Ahead of the Midterms
item.description: In this opinion article, former U.S. Labor Secretary Robert Reich argues that former President Donald Trump is promoting claims of voter fraud as a strategy to influence future elections and strengthen support for new voting restrictions. Reich contends that documented cases of voter fraud, including voting by noncitizens, are extremely rare and that concerns about widespread fraud are being exaggerated for political purposes. He focuses on three developments he believes could affect upcoming midterm elections. First, he criticizes proposals that would require voters to provide proof of citizenship, such as passports or certified birth certificates, arguing that many eligible Americans may have difficulty obtaining or presenting these documents. He also opposes efforts to restrict mail-in voting, noting that millions of Americans have used this voting method. Second, Reich expresses concern about reports that federal immigration or border enforcement personnel could be deployed near po

item.title: Media Analyst Says Scott Pelley’s Dismissal Could Trigger Newsroom Backlash and Legal Disputes at CBS
item.description: The article discusses reactions to the reported firing of veteran CBS journalist Scott Pelley and the potential consequences for the network. According to the report, CNN media analyst Brian Stelter described the situation as an 'underwater earthquake' within CBS News, suggesting that the effects could extend well beyond the immediate personnel decision. Pelley, a longtime journalist who spent more than four decades at CBS News and was closely associated with the program '60 Minutes,' was reportedly dismissed one day after publicly criticizing network leadership and accusing executives of damaging the news program. The termination was carried out by CBS Executive Producer Nick Bilton, who reportedly fired Pelley 'for cause,' a designation that may have important legal implications. Stelter argued during an appearance on CNN’s 'The Source' with Kaitlan Collins that the decision is unlikely to be received positively by many employees within CBS News. He suggested that the firing could de

item.title: Senate hearing turns contentious as Treasury Secretary Bessent and Sen. Wyden clash over Epstein-related allegations
item.description:
A Senate hearing on Wednesday became highly contentious after a heated exchange between U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Sen. Ron Wyden. The confrontation centered on allegations related to Jeffrey Epstein and accusations involving financial records and past business connections.
Sen. Wyden, who has been involved in ongoing congressional scrutiny of Epstein-related financial activity, accused the Treasury Department under Bessent of failing to properly disclose or investigate relevant financial documents tied to Epstein’s network. In response, Bessent pushed back strongly, rejecting the accusation and accusing Wyden of political motives and misconduct.
