Meanwhile, the US military has demonstrated readiness to resume combat operations in the Persian Gulf if required, highlighting the strategic importance of the region. In the Strait of Hormuz, a Gambian-flagged vessel en route to Iran was disabled by US forces for violating the blockade, and Omani authorities reported a suspected naval mine, raising further security concerns. In Lebanon, clashes between Hezbollah and Israeli forces have intensified despite a prior US-mediated ceasefire, leading to school closures and hospital relocations in northern Israel. Gas prices in the US have slightly decreased but remain significantly higher than bef

item.title: Researchers Propose Liver as a Potential Magnetoreceptor in Pigeons
item.description: For decades, scientists have been puzzled by the ability of homing pigeons to navigate back to their lofts from hundreds of miles away with remarkable accuracy. Traditional theories of avian navigation have focused on magnetoreception, with candidate organs including magnetite in the beak, cryptochrome proteins in the retina, and hair cells in the inner ear. None of these has been conclusively proven as the primary sensor. A recent study suggests a surprising alternative: the liver. Researchers hypothesize that iron-rich cells in the liver, which naturally store iron in forms like ferritin and hemosiderin, could detect variations in the Earth's magnetic field. This information could then be converted into electrochemical signals to assist navigation. While this is physiologically plausible, the study acknowledges that the pathway from liver to brain remains unproven, and birds likely integrate multiple sensory cues including visual, olfactory, and infrasound signals. If hepatic magneto

item.title: Reporter and Pentagon correspondent dispute Trump official’s claims over UFC ticket outreach to U.S. troops
item.description:
A public dispute emerged between Washington Post Pentagon reporter Dan Lamothe and Sean Parnell, Assistant Secretary of the Army for Manpower and Reserve Affairs, over reporting related to UFC ticket distribution involving U.S. troops. The controversy centers on claims that service members were being encouraged to attend a UFC event associated with the White House, with some reporting suggesting troops were asked to cover their own travel expenses and that attendance was limited by certain eligibility criteria.
Parnell criticized the original reporting, describing it as misleading and arguing that the White House and UFC were instead offering a valuable opportunity for service members and their families to attend a unique, once-in-a-lifetime experience. He framed the initiative as generous and beneficial to military personnel, rejecting the negative characterization implied in the story.
Reporter Dan Lamothe responded publicly on social media, stating that Parnell’s team had declined

item.title: Kelsea Ballerini Shares Photos in Sheer Metallic Dress and Updates Fans on Music and Life
item.description: Country singer Kelsea Ballerini recently shared a series of Instagram photos highlighting her life and career, including a striking image of her in a long-sleeve metallic sheer dress with web-like patterns. The dress revealed her dark bra and high-waisted underwear, and she completed the look with soft makeup and a half-up, half-down hairstyle. The photo was part of a larger slideshow showing Ballerini in the studio, traveling with friends, attending a Broadway show, and enjoying casual moments. Fans expressed excitement about her appearance and upcoming music projects. Ballerini hinted at new music by posting a text exchange with Patrick Droney that included an audio file titled 'Math of Us,' suggesting collaboration and creative progress. Her last full-length album, released in October 2024, included hits such as 'Cowboys Cry Too' and 'Baggage,' with a deluxe edition following in March 2025. On a personal note, Ballerini and actor Chase Stokes experienced another breakup after briefl

item.title: Pete Davidson Praises Kim Kardashian’s Work Ethic and Acting Skills on Podcast Appearance
item.description:
Pete Davidson spoke positively about his ex-girlfriend Kim Kardashian during a recent appearance on his podcast alongside comedian Nikki Glaser. Although Davidson and Kardashian ended their nine-month relationship in 2022, he referred to her in highly complimentary terms, describing her as “superhuman” for her ability to stay focused and productive despite public scrutiny and ongoing media attention. He emphasized that she has a strong ability to compartmentalize personal and public issues in order to get work done efficiently, a trait he said he learned from observing her.
Davidson also praised Kardashian’s acting abilities, recalling conversations they had during their relationship in which she expressed interest in pursuing acting. He supported her ambitions and noted that she is talented in the field, referencing her appearances in television and film projects such as FX’s “American Horror Story: Delicate” and Hulu’s “All’s Fair.” During the podcast, Nikki Glaser also joined in th

item.title: WHO Director-General discusses Ebola containment efforts in the Democratic Republic of Congo during CNN interview
item.description:
CNN’s Clarissa Ward conducted an in-depth interview with Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the Director-General of the World Health Organization, while aboard a plane traveling over the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The conversation focused on the ongoing response to Ebola concerns in the region and the international efforts aimed at preventing the disease from spreading further. During the interview, Dr. Tedros addressed the challenges faced by health authorities and humanitarian teams working in affected areas, emphasizing the importance of rapid response, coordination with local governments, and sustained global support. He highlighted that controlling Ebola outbreaks requires not only medical intervention but also strong surveillance systems, community engagement, and logistical coordination in often difficult and remote environments.
The discussion underscored the WHO’s role in supporting national health systems and deploying resources where needed most quickly, particularly in

item.title: Pennsylvania Prepares for Medicaid Work Requirements Amid Administrative and Coverage Concerns
item.description:
Pennsylvania is preparing to implement new Medicaid work requirements that will take effect in January 2027 under a federal mandate. State officials say the changes will significantly increase administrative responsibilities, requiring extensive employee training, technology upgrades, and additional staffing. According to Pennsylvania Department of Human Services Secretary Val Arkoosh, approximately 6,000 county assistance office employees will need retraining, and around 250 new workers may be hired to manage the increased workload. The state also expects to spend about $50 million on system upgrades.
Under the new rules, many Medicaid recipients between ages 19 and 64 who do not have children under 14 must demonstrate at least 80 hours per month of work, education, or volunteer activity to maintain coverage. Several groups are exempt, including pregnant women, medically frail individuals, certain veterans, former foster youth, caregivers of people with disabilities, individuals rece

item.title: Colorado GOP gubernatorial candidate declines to answer question about claims of past killings in interview
item.description:
Victor Marx, the Republican frontrunner for Colorado governor, drew attention after refusing to answer a question about how many people he has killed during a televised interview with local news outlet 9News. When pressed by reporter Kyle Clark, Marx responded with the deflective question, “Does it matter?”, which quickly gained attention online. The exchange occurred during a 30-minute interview ahead of the 2026 election cycle.
Marx has built a public persona around what he describes as a tough and dangerous personal history, including claims that he was forced to kill a man at the age of seven and other alleged killings tied to his work as a “high risk missionary.” He has also promoted a self-styled identity he refers to as the “Dangerous Gentleman,” which is also the title of his book. However, questions have been raised about the accuracy and consistency of his accounts.
The reporting notes that Marx served in the military but did not deploy or see combat, prompting further scru

item.title: Boebert says election-denier Tina Peters should not access federal compensation fund
item.description: Congresswoman Lauren Boebert (R-CO) publicly stated her opposition to the idea of using federal funds intended to compensate those wronged by the government to benefit Tina Peters, a Colorado election denier recently released from prison. Peters, a former county clerk, was convicted in 2024 for tampering with election equipment and initially sentenced to nine years in prison. However, after a commutation by Democratic Governor Jared Polis, she is being released in under two years, following pressure from former President Donald Trump. Boebert emphasized that federal funds, including the $1.8 billion anti-weaponization fund associated with Trump, should be reserved for individuals harmed by the federal government, not those convicted of state-level crimes. She argued that any restitution for Peters should come from state mechanisms, not federal resources. Peters' conviction and early release have stirred political debate, highlighting the continuing controversies surrounding election de

item.title: Conservative activist criticizes Michele Bachmann over claim linking divine intervention to GOP midterm success
item.description:
Former Republican congresswoman Michele Bachmann sparked controversy after asserting that God would ensure Republican victories in the upcoming midterm elections if former President Donald Trump continues military action against Iran. In a video that circulated widely online, Bachmann framed current geopolitical events as a “biblical moment,” suggesting that both Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu were divinely appointed for their roles. She claimed that decisive action against Iran would lead not only to Republican electoral success in the United States but also to favorable political outcomes in Israel.
Her remarks drew sharp criticism from David Pyne, a prominent figure behind the America First Conservative account on X, who had previously supported Bachmann during her 2012 presidential campaign. Pyne strongly rejected her statements, describing them as extreme and disconnected from the nationalist wing of the Republican Party. He criticized both the notion of divi

item.title: Trump criticizes court ruling over Kennedy Center name removal and withdraws support for project
item.description:
Former U.S. President Donald Trump reacted strongly after a federal judge ordered his name removed from the Kennedy Center building and blocked a proposed temporary closure tied to renovation plans. In a lengthy post on Truth Social, Trump declared that the Kennedy Center would face both structural and financial collapse without his involvement, claiming that he had intended to invest “hundreds of millions of dollars” in the institution’s renovation and revitalization. He argued that the venue has long been “broken” and “unsafe,” and suggested that his participation was essential for its successful transformation.
Trump also announced that he was withdrawing from any involvement in the project following the court decision. He criticized U.S. District Judge Christopher Cooper, who issued the ruling, and accused him of obstructing efforts to improve the cultural institution. In his comments, Trump further escalated the dispute by questioning the judge’s impartiality and referencing the

item.title: Hawaii Congressional Candidate Kirill Basin Charged After Allegedly Threatening County Employees With a Firearm
item.description:
Kirill Basin, a Democratic candidate for Congress in Hawaii, was arrested and charged with Terroristic Threatening in the First Degree following an incident at a Maui County government building. According to reports, Basin allegedly brandished a firearm during an argument with county employees inside the facility. Authorities stated that although police were not immediately called, officers later arrested him and initiated criminal proceedings.
Maui Police Chief John Pelletier said the department treats incidents involving threats and public safety concerns with the utmost seriousness. The allegations emerged amid reports that Basin had been experiencing personal and professional turmoil. Earlier the same day as his arrest, police had reportedly escorted him from a public town hall event.
The situation was further complicated by a restraining order request filed by Basin's executive assistant, Jared Agtunong. Court filings cited repeated harassing phone calls and text messages allege

item.title: Wisconsin Democrats test socialist-style economic populism in a swing state
item.description:
The article examines how democratic socialism and broader economic populism are becoming a bigger force in Wisconsin politics ahead of the 2026 midterms. It focuses on Francesca Hong, a progressive candidate who has emerged as an early frontrunner in the Democratic primary for governor in a state that Donald Trump narrowly won in 2024. Hong’s platform includes free child care, a $20 minimum wage, a moratorium on data center construction, and strong criticism of the Israeli government, which has helped define both her appeal and the backlash she faces.
The piece places Hong within a wider national trend in which left-leaning candidates are gaining confidence in competitive states by emphasizing affordability, labor rights, and public investment. It notes that Wisconsin has its own historical connection to socialism, including past socialist mayors in Milwaukee and Bernie Sanders’s strong showing in the state in 2016. That local history helps explain why some Democrats believe more open

item.title: Debate over abortion stance by Georgia GOP candidate sparks concerns about electoral impact
item.description:
Former Republican Congressman Joe Walsh criticized Georgia Lt. Gov. Burt Jones after a resurfaced video appeared to show Jones supporting a total abortion ban during a past GOP debate. The footage, reportedly from 2022, contrasts with more moderate positions such as six-week abortion restrictions that have been adopted by some Republicans. Walsh argued that Jones’ stance could politically benefit Democrats in Georgia, a state that has become increasingly competitive in recent election cycles.
Walsh publicly reacted on social media, suggesting that such a hardline position on abortion may not resonate with the broader electorate in Georgia. He warned that this could provide Democrats with a strategic advantage in the upcoming gubernatorial race. His comments reflect ongoing divisions within the Republican Party over how to approach abortion policy following the Supreme Court’s 2022 decision to overturn Roe v. Wade.
Georgia has shifted politically in recent years, with Democrats winnin

item.title: Experts Warn DOJ Actions Could Undermine State Bar Oversight
item.description: Legal experts and journalists are raising concerns over recent actions by the U.S. Department of Justice under Acting Attorney Todd Blanche. According to reporting by Adam Klasfeld, Blanche has allegedly sought to intimidate state and local bar associations, which are responsible for licensing and disciplining lawyers. This includes suing the D.C. Bar and attempting to limit state bars' authority over DOJ lawyers. The Campaign for Accountability, which filed a bar complaint against Blanche, reports that these efforts are creating a 'chilling effect' on bar associations, potentially deterring them from investigating misconduct by DOJ attorneys. The pattern reportedly began under former U.S. Attorney Pam Bondi, who implemented a policy of non-cooperation with bar investigations, and has intensified since Blanche assumed leadership. Critics warn that these moves may weaken the independence of bar associations and hinder their role in holding lawyers accountable, raising concerns about ove

item.title: Trump endorses Republican governor candidates in South Carolina, Iowa, and Oklahoma
item.description:
President Donald Trump intervened in three contested Republican gubernatorial primaries by endorsing candidates in South Carolina, Iowa, and Oklahoma. In a series of social media posts, he backed South Carolina Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette, Iowa Rep. Randy Feenstra, and former Oklahoma state senator Mike Mazzei. The endorsements came as each state’s primary campaign was nearing its decisive stage, making Trump’s support a central factor in races where candidates have been competing hard for his approval.
The article highlights how Trump’s backing can shape Republican primary politics, especially in states where loyalty to him remains a major test for candidates seeking the party’s nomination. In South Carolina, Iowa, and Oklahoma, the contests had already been defined by efforts to win over Trump-aligned voters and activists. His endorsements effectively signaled which candidates he believed were best positioned to carry his agenda and political brand into the general election.
The piece a

item.title: Shenzhou 22 Returns to Earth Carrying Shenzhou 21 Crew After Extended Tiangong Mission
item.description:
The Shenzhou 22 spacecraft has successfully returned to Earth carrying the crew of Shenzhou 21, marking the end of a prolonged mission aboard China’s Tiangong space station. The landing took place on May 29, 2026, at the Dongfeng landing site in Inner Mongolia. Onboard were commander Zhang Lu, flight engineer Wu Fei, and payload specialist Zhang Hongzhang, who had originally launched on October 31, 2025, aboard Shenzhou 21. The mission lasted 210 days in orbit, setting a new endurance record for Chinese space missions and surpassing the previous record held by the Shenzhou 20 crew. This mission was notable not only for its duration but also for the operational adjustments required during a recent spacecraft anomaly involving Shenzhou 20, which had developed suspected window damage. As a result, crew rotations were modified, and multiple spacecraft were used in unconventional return configurations across the program.
During their stay aboard Tiangong, the astronauts conducted extensive

item.title: Steelers part ways with special teams coordinator Derrius Swinton
item.description:
The Pittsburgh Steelers have made a coaching staff change by parting ways with special teams coordinator Derrius Swinton. According to the report, Swinton has been relieved of his duties after serving on the team’s coaching staff. The move reflects ongoing adjustments within the organization as it evaluates its performance across all phases of the game, including special teams, which play a critical role in field position, kicking game execution, and overall game management.
While specific details regarding the reasoning behind the decision were not fully outlined in the provided text, coaching changes in the NFL often follow internal reviews of unit performance over the course of the season. Special teams performance can significantly influence close games, and teams frequently reassess coaching responsibilities when expectations are not met or when broader strategic changes are being implemented.
Derrius Swinton, who has experience coaching special teams across multiple NFL franchi

item.title: Mississippi Senate Race Tightens as Democrat Scott Colom Gains Ground Against Cindy Hyde-Smith
item.description:
A recent report highlights growing political tension in Mississippi as the state's Republican Party acknowledges concern over a competitive U.S. Senate race. Democratic candidate Scott Colom, a district attorney and lifelong Mississippian, is reportedly gaining unexpected traction against incumbent Republican Senator Cindy Hyde-Smith. According to campaign communications cited in the report, the chair of the Mississippi Republican Party warned fellow Republicans against complacency, emphasizing that the state cannot assume automatic victories based on its traditionally strong Republican alignment.
Polling data referenced in the article suggests the race has tightened significantly, with some surveys indicating a statistical tie between the candidates. This development has drawn attention from national political observers, as Mississippi has not elected a Democrat to the U.S. Senate in decades. Colom’s campaign is described as a grassroots effort, focusing on local engagement and voter

item.title: Debate over U.S. radiation safety standards and regulatory changes under the Trump administration
item.description:
The article examines a controversial shift in U.S. nuclear safety policy, arguing that changes promoted during the Trump administration could weaken long-standing radiation protection standards. It focuses on the potential rollback of the Linear No-Threshold (LNT) model and the ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable) principle, both of which have guided radiation exposure limits for workers and the public since the 1970s. These frameworks assume that any amount of ionizing radiation carries some risk of cancer, and that exposure should be minimized whenever feasible based on cost-benefit analysis.
According to the analysis, a 2025 executive order directed the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to reconsider reliance on these models, challenging the scientific basis for assuming no safe threshold of radiation exposure. Critics of the change warn that replacing LNT-based standards with alternative models, such as radiation hormesis or fixed exposure thresholds, could reduce safety marg

item.title: Trump Faces Criticism from Supporters Over America 250 Speech Decision
item.description: Following the withdrawal of several artists from the America 250 celebration marking the nation's 250th anniversary, President Donald Trump's decision to replace the canceled performances with a speech has drawn criticism, even from his most loyal supporters. The White House branded the event as the 'AMERICA IS BACK Rally,' but conservative commentators expressed disappointment, describing the move as self-serving and uninspired. Right-wing media personality Matt Walsh criticized the replacement of a concert with a speech, suggesting that the administration could have invited lesser-known musicians eager to perform. Jenna Ellis, a former Trump campaign lawyer, called the decision egotistical, emphasizing that the celebration is meant to honor the country rather than Trump himself. Similarly, commentator Jackie Chea recommended allowing new artists to perform patriotic songs instead of turning the event into a personal rally. The controversy highlights ongoing tensions within Trump's ba

item.title: James Carville rejects comparison between Democrats and January 6 rioters during CNN interview
item.description:
Democratic political strategist James Carville appeared on CNN with host Michael Smerconish, where he forcefully rejected a comparison drawn between some Democratic political attitudes and the mindset of individuals involved in the January 6 Capitol attack. The discussion began as Carville emphasized what he sees as the need for Democrats to adopt more aggressive and outspoken political tactics, including defending public figures such as musician Bruce Springsteen for his criticism of Donald Trump and supporting controversial political candidates.
During the exchange, Carville also made a provocative remark, stating that he has “Trump Derangement Syndrome” and is attempting to intensify it, adding that he believes Springsteen shares similar views. Smerconish argued that certain political motivations within the Democratic Party reflect a “blind eye” to internal shortcomings, referencing a Maine Senate candidate with a controversial past as part of his critique. He further suggested tha

item.title: Trump criticizes Chicago mayor during Vatican meeting between Pope Leo XIV and Mayor Brandon Johnson
item.description:
President Donald Trump sparked controversy after posting a message on Truth Social criticizing Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson while Johnson was attending a meeting at the Vatican with Pope Leo XIV. In his post, Trump called the mayor “useless” and suggested that someone should inform the pope of that opinion. He also abruptly added a statement about Iran and nuclear weapons, shifting the focus from local U.S. politics to international security concerns in a single remark. The post was signed with his full presidential title, reinforcing its official tone.
Mayor Johnson had shared photos of his Vatican visit on social media, describing the encounter with Pope Leo XIV as one of the most meaningful experiences of his life. He referred to the pontiff as a “magnificent human” and emphasized the honor of participating in a broader interfaith prayer gathering. According to Johnson’s office, the meeting was part of a larger religious and diplomatic event, and not a political summit. Trump was

item.title: ICE Explores Using Digital Advertising Data to Track Immigrants
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) is reportedly exploring new methods to monitor and target immigrants by requesting information on how to access data from digital advertisers. This data could include users' social media activity, online purchases, and web browsing habits. According to Politico, this is the first public request of its kind by ICE, raising concerns among privacy advocates and tech industry leaders. Brian O'Kelley, CEO of an AI advertising company, expressed alarm, stating that the use of digital advertising ecosystems to track individuals is deeply concerning and potentially invasive. ICE representatives framed the initiative as part of their mission under the current administration to identify and remove dangerous individuals from the U.S., emphasizing that all actions would be within legal boundaries. Critics argue that the initiative could lead to broader surveillance, especially given the political climate and fears of retribution under the Trump admini

item.title: Judge Kathleen Williams Orders Review of Trump IRS Settlement Over Alleged Fraud
item.description: On Friday, Judge Kathleen Williams took a significant step by ordering a review of former President Donald Trump’s $10 billion case against the IRS, citing potential fraud in the settlement process. The decision follows a filing from 35 former federal judges urging the case to be reopened to investigate whether the settlement, designed to end the lawsuit quickly, was based on deceptive practices. The judges claim the agreement allowed Trump and his associates to receive financial benefits improperly while circumventing constitutional and judicial oversight. Judge Williams emphasized that attorneys must not file documents for improper purposes, and a lawsuit used solely to force a settlement could qualify as such. She also questioned the settlement’s legality, noting that an addendum waiving tax claims against Trump and his family was signed only by the acting Attorney General, Todd Blanche, potentially exposing him to legal consequences. The case draws parallels to historical misconduc

item.title: White House denies report claiming advisers urged JD Vance to reduce social media activity
item.description:
The White House strongly denied a New York Times report alleging that Vice President JD Vance had been advised by senior officials, including Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, to take a step back from his social media activity. According to the Times, Wiles and other unnamed West Wing officials had reportedly encouraged Vance to reduce his online presence due to concerns that some of his posts and engagements were considered inappropriate for his office. The report suggested that this advice was part of broader internal discussions about managing communications discipline within the administration.
White House Communications Director Steven Cheung forcefully rejected the story, stating on X that the report was false and that the administration had already denied the claims to the New York Times. He accused the newspaper of refusing to include the White House’s response and characterized the reporting as “fake news.” Another Trump adviser, Alex Bruesewitz, also defended Vance and echoed the

item.title: Ken Paxton faces public scrutiny over past courthouse incident as divorce trial approaches
item.description: Texas Attorney General-turned-Senate candidate Ken Paxton is set to appear at his upcoming divorce trial, attracting renewed attention due to a previous incident at the same courthouse. In 2013, Paxton took a $1,000 luxury fountain pen that had been mistakenly left at a security checkpoint. The incident was recorded on security cameras and, after a review by the Collin County Sheriff’s Office, Paxton promptly returned the item, with his spokesperson describing it as a simple mistake. Nonetheless, the political action committee Lone Star Rising issued a cautionary notice to those attending the trial, warning them to watch their valuables. Former Democratic state Representative Wendy Davis also highlighted the incident on social media, emphasizing that Paxton had previously taken a valuable pen at the courthouse. Paxton’s divorce comes after his wife filed on “biblical grounds,” citing infidelity, while Paxton himself faced accusations of an affair during his 2023 impeachment trial. Alth

item.title: Graham Platner Faces Scrutiny Over Explicit Texts Revealed by Wife
item.description: Democratic U.S. Senate candidate Graham Platner of Maine is facing increased scrutiny after reports revealed that his wife, Amy Gertner, discovered sexually explicit text messages on his phone. According to the Wall Street Journal, Gertner found the messages in spring 2025 and later shared her findings with a campaign aide while the team was conducting opposition research on Platner. Campaign aides reportedly viewed the texts as a private matter and did not see them as a threat to his political career. Platner and his wife have been in counseling, and Gertner stated that their marriage is currently stronger than ever. Platner is already dealing with several controversies, including a Nazi-linked tattoo, previous comments about sexual assault victims, and content from his deleted Reddit account, which expressed lenient views on infidelity. These issues have added to the challenges of his campaign as he seeks to unseat incumbent Republican Senator Susan Collins. Despite these personal co

item.title: CBS defends replacing Stephen Colbert with Byron Allen despite poor ratings
item.description: CBS recently replaced Stephen Colbert's long-running 'The Late Show' with Byron Allen's 'Comics Unleashed,' a move the network claims was financially strategic. CBS stated that switching to a 'time buy' model transformed a loss of roughly $40 million annually into a $15 million profit, representing a $55 million financial swing. Critics, including fellow late-night host Jimmy Kimmel, challenged this justification, calling the claimed losses for Colbert's show 'nonsensical.' The debut episode of 'Comics Unleashed' drew only 995,000 viewers, trailing behind reruns of shows by Kimmel and Jimmy Fallon, which each attracted over 1.5 million viewers. Additionally, Colbert's continued online presence remains strong; a recent appearance on 'Only in Monroe' garnered 928,000 YouTube views alone. CBS's statement framed the change as a calculated financial move, but public reception and viewership figures suggest the new program has struggled to maintain the audience that Colbert built over decade

item.title: Yoga Helps Improve Mood, Fatigue, and Sleep in Cancer Survivors, Study Finds
item.description: A recent clinical trial suggests that cancer survivors can experience significant physical and emotional benefits through a structured yoga program. The study, published by the American Society of Clinical Oncology, involved 410 participants, mostly female breast-cancer survivors. Half of the participants received standard post-treatment care, while the other half engaged in the four-week Yoga for Cancer Survivors (YOCAS) program. This program combines hatha and restorative yoga, emphasizing gentle movements, breathing exercises, and mindfulness. Participants in the YOCAS group practiced about 180 minutes per week over three sessions. By the end of the trial, they reported notable improvements in mood, anxiety, fatigue, and insomnia compared to those who received standard care alone. Experts note that yoga offers a non-pharmaceutical approach to alleviating multiple post-treatment challenges simultaneously. Licensed clinical social workers and supportive oncology directors emphasize th

item.title: Political strategist warns Trump is losing influence and facing growing opposition within GOP
item.description: Steve Schmidt, a Republican strategist known for managing John McCain's 2008 presidential campaign, has issued a stark assessment of former President Donald Trump's political standing. In his recent Substack newsletter, Schmidt argues that Trump's influence over the Republican Party is weakening and highlights evidence suggesting that his perceived invincibility is eroding. According to Schmidt, Trump's power has historically been rooted more in fear than ideology, patriotism, or conservative principles, with allies fearing retaliation, humiliation, or political fallout. Schmidt points to several developments as signs of this decline: a federal judge blocking Trump's $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund, ongoing legal challenges to tariff schemes, resistance to White House projects, and conflicts such as the Kennedy Center dispute. He also notes public actions by GOP senators like Thom Tillis and John Cornyn as indications that party members are beginning to calculate independently o

item.title: California health officials warn against foraging wild mushrooms amid record poisoning cases
item.description:
California public health officials are urging residents to avoid picking or consuming wild mushrooms following a sharp and unprecedented rise in poisoning cases across the state. Since November, authorities have identified 50 cases linked primarily to highly toxic species such as the death cap (Amanita phalloides) and the western destroying angel (Amanita ocreata). These mushrooms are among the most dangerous in the world, with the death cap alone responsible for the majority of fatal mushroom poisonings globally. In California, the recent surge has already resulted in four deaths and four cases requiring liver transplants, marking one of the most severe outbreaks in the state’s history.
Officials note that in a typical year, the California Poison Control System records only a handful of mushroom-related illnesses, making the current spike especially alarming. The outbreak has been concentrated in Northern California and the Central Coast, including counties such as Alameda, Humboldt,

item.title: Coco Gauff eliminated in third round of Roland Garros by Anastasia Potapova
item.description: Coco Gauff, the defending champion at Roland Garros, suffered an unexpected defeat in the third round, losing to Austria's Anastasia Potapova. The fourth-seeded Gauff fell 4-6, 7-6, 6-4 in a two-and-a-half-hour match. Despite a strong comeback from a 5-2 deficit in the second set, Gauff was unable to secure the set in the tiebreaker and ultimately lost the deciding set. This marks Gauff’s worst performance at Roland Garros since her debut in 2020 and follows a series of challenging results in recent grand slam tournaments, including early exits at Wimbledon and the US Open. Potapova, the 28th seed, advanced to the fourth round for the first time since 2024, equaling her best Roland Garros performance. The match was part of a difficult day for American women in Paris, with Amanda Anisimova also being eliminated. Gauff had previously made it to the quarterfinals at the Australian Open this year but had higher hopes for her return to the famous clay courts of Roland Garros. Her loss, alon

item.title: Jake Tapper and critics react to Trump suggesting himself as entertainment for a state fair event
item.description:
According to the article, U.S. President Donald Trump sparked widespread reactions after proposing that he personally replace musical performers at a planned event tied to the Great American State Fair. The proposal came after several scheduled artists reportedly withdrew from the concert lineup, an event organized by a Trump-linked group called Freedom 250. Trump characterized the departures as performers getting “the yips” and suggested that he could instead serve as the main attraction, rebranding the appearance as an “America is Back Rally” where he would deliver a major speech rather than perform traditional music acts.
The idea quickly drew attention and criticism online, including from CNN anchor Jake Tapper, who remarked that he initially thought the post was not real and expressed concern about the political implications of the situation. Tapper also noted that the withdrawal of artists appeared tied to concerns that the event was becoming politically partisan, raising questi

item.title: Democratic Representative Calls Trump an Adjudicated Rapist on MSNBC, Host Issues Terminology Clarification
item.description: During a Saturday afternoon segment on MSNBC's MS NOW, host Alex Witt interviewed Rep. Melanie Stansbury (D-NM) about former Attorney General Pam Bondi's combative appearance before the House Oversight Committee. The conversation turned to reports that the Trump administration's Department of Justice may investigate E. Jean Carroll, the writer who successfully sued Donald Trump for sexual abuse. Stansbury stated, 'Donald Trump, we all know that you are an adjudicated rapist, and now you're going after survivors that have held you accountable.' She argued this reflects an effort to weaponize the Justice Department against critics, comparing it to the actions that led to Richard Nixon's downfall. Witt quickly interjected to clarify the legal terminology, noting that Trump was found liable for sexual abuse rather than rape under the specific New York Penal Law definition. However, the summary referenced Judge Lewis Kaplan's remarks, where he explained that while the jury did not find rape

item.title: Five People Rescued from Laos Cave After Over a Week Trapped
item.description: A daring rescue operation in Laos successfully brought five people out of a remote cave after they had been trapped for more than a week. The mission, described as complex and challenging, involved specialized cave divers navigating deep and narrow passages to reach the stranded individuals. Rescue teams are still searching for two other people who remain unaccounted for, carefully assessing the situation and planning the next steps to ensure their safe extraction. The operation has been documented through detailed diagrams and images provided by the Metta Tham Kalasin Command and Control Center, as well as photographs from Reuters and Facebook. This incident highlights both the risks involved in cave exploration and the remarkable coordination and expertise required for such high-stakes rescues.

item.title: Kaitlan Collins Confirms Memo on Military Weight and Fitness Requirements for White House UFC Event
item.description: CNN journalist Kaitlan Collins fact-checked Scott Jennings, a commentator with MAGA-friendly views, after he denied reports that the Defense Department set specific weight and height requirements for military members attending President Donald Trump’s White House UFC event. Jennings claimed that the White House told him the reports were 'fake news' and that there were no restrictions for military attendees. Collins countered, referencing an internal memo that clearly outlined the requirements: ticket recipients must meet the Department of War waist-to-height ratio standard of less than 0.55 and comply with all service-specific physical fitness tests. For the average soldier, this meant a maximum waist of 37 inches for men and 35 inches for women. Defense officials confirmed that the intention behind these standards was to ensure that military personnel 'look good' on camera, with one bluntly describing the policy as 'No fatties.' The controversy raised concerns about the use of militar

item.title: Candid 1980s Photos of Michael Jackson With Hollywood and Music Icons
item.description:
This article presents a curated gallery of candid photographs of Michael Jackson taken during the 1980s, a period widely recognized as the peak of his global fame. At the time, Jackson had already become one of the most influential and recognizable entertainers in the world, following the massive success of albums like “Thriller” and his growing dominance in music, television, and live performances. The images featured in the collection highlight his interactions with numerous high-profile celebrities from entertainment, film, and music.
Throughout the gallery, Michael Jackson is seen alongside a wide range of iconic figures, including Elizabeth Taylor, Diana Ross, Whitney Houston, Sophia Loren, Lionel Richie, Liza Minnelli, Cher, Brooke Shields, Olivia Newton-John, Eddie Murphy, Yul Brynner, and others. These photographs capture informal and public moments that reflect both his professional connections and personal friendships with some of the most famous individuals of the era.

item.title: University of Illinois Develops Scalable 3D Silicon Chip Technology for Higher Computing Density
item.description: Researchers at the University of Illinois Grainger College of Engineering have developed a new method for building three-dimensional (3D) silicon chips that could significantly extend the progress predicted by Moore’s Law. Traditional chip miniaturization is approaching physical limits, but this breakthrough uses ultrathin silicon membranes and low-temperature manufacturing techniques to stack multiple layers of silicon circuits directly on top of one another. This approach enables faster communication between layers, higher device density, and reduced energy consumption. The team fabricated three stacked layers of transistors using junctionless designs, achieving performance comparable to conventional silicon chips while remaining within the thermal limits required for monolithic 3D integration. Unlike existing 3D chip technologies that bond completed wafers, this method produces layers sequentially on a single wafer, allowing nanometer-scale alignment and denser vertical interconnect

item.title: United Airlines flight diverted to Madison after passenger attempts to access cockpit
item.description:
A United Airlines flight traveling from Chicago to Minneapolis was forced to divert to Madison, Wisconsin, after a disruptive passenger allegedly attempted multiple times to breach the cockpit, according to airline officials and air traffic control audio reviewed by CNN. The incident occurred aboard United flight 2005, a Boeing 737 carrying 147 passengers and six crew members.
Crew members reported that the passenger made repeated attempts to approach or access the cockpit during the flight, prompting immediate intervention. According to accounts from passengers and recorded communications, law enforcement officers who were onboard the aircraft intervened and restrained the individual before the situation escalated further. The passenger was then seated and monitored under the supervision of officers on board.
Prior to takeoff, there were already signs of unusual behavior. The passenger reportedly stood up during taxi at Chicago O’Hare International Airport and had to be instructed m

item.title: Trump Defends NFL Rookie Jaxson Dart After Backlash Over Presidential Introduction
item.description:
Former U.S. President Donald Trump publicly defended New York Giants rookie quarterback Jaxson Dart following criticism over Dart’s recent participation in a political event. According to the report, Dart, who played 14 games in his first NFL season and was part of a struggling Giants team that finished with a 4–13 record, faced backlash after unexpectedly introducing Trump at a New York event last week. The appearance reportedly surprised some team officials and teammates, with sports media noting that the original plan did not include Dart in that role. Instead, it appears his involvement was added later, which contributed to internal confusion within the organization.
The situation sparked debate among fans and commentators, leading to criticism directed at the young quarterback. In response, Trump addressed the controversy on his social media platform, posting a message that praised Dart and dismissed his critics. In his post, Trump thanked Dart for the interaction, suggested that

item.title: Trump criticizes judge over Kennedy Center renovation ruling
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a lengthy statement on social media Saturday criticizing a federal judge's ruling to halt renovations at the Kennedy Center and remove his name from the project. U.S. District Judge Casey Cooper determined that the renovations violated legal authority, citing Congress’ role in renaming the performing arts center. In his post, Trump accused the judge of having a conflict of interest because his wife, Amy Jeffress, previously worked as a Justice Department prosecutor and as a personal attorney for former President Joe Biden. Trump called Jeffress a 'radical left Democrat' and claimed that her connections made it impossible for him to receive fair treatment. He also suggested that the judge should face criminal charges for not disclosing these connections. This social media outburst follows a similar statement on Friday, where Trump attacked Cooper as a judge 'appointed by Barack Hussein Obama.'
